instruction:
Write a complete testthat test file for `usethis::use_addin`.

Package: usethis v3.2.1.9000
Description: Automate Package and Project Setup
Function: use_addin
Parameters: addin, open
Test patterns used: test_that
Expectations used: expect_equal

Function Code:
```r
use_addin <- function(addin = "new_addin", open = rlang::is_interactive()) {
  addin_dcf_path <- proj_path("inst", "rstudio", "addins.dcf")

  if (!file_exists(addin_dcf_path)) {
    create_directory(proj_path("inst", "rstudio"))
    file_create(addin_dcf_path)
    ui_bullets(c("v" = "Creating {.path {pth(addin_dcf_path)}}"))
  }

  addin_info <- render_template("addins.dcf", data = list(addin = addin))
  addin_info[length(addin_info) + 1] <- ""
  write_utf8(addin_dcf_path, addin_info, append = TRUE)
  ui_bullets(c(
    "v" = "Adding binding to {.fun {addin}} to {.path addins.dcf}"
  ))

  if (open) {
    edit_file(addin_dcf_path)
  }

  invisible(TRUE)
}
```

Requirements:
1. Use test_that() blocks with descriptive names that reference `use_addin`
2. Test basic functionality using appropriate expectations (expect_equal)
3. Test edge cases: NULL input, empty strings, missing arguments
4. Test error conditions with expect_error()
5. Each test_that() block should test one logical behavior
